notes: Codex is the FORK variant of checkpoint-rewind. Codex's Source is FilesUnderRoot(~/.codex/sessions): a proc-<PID> pre-session appears at launch, hands off to a rollout-keyed UUID-1 session on the first prompt. UUID-1 runs TWO normal turns ('one', 'two') — the checkpoints to rewind back from. Then /fork EAGERLY materializes a SECOND rollout (UUID-2) with a fresh session_id carrying the replayed pre-fork history; the original UUID-1 rollout lingers frozen (codex emits NO transcript_removed at fork time, the old row stays ready until process_exited). The post-fork turn ('three') runs on UUID-2 from the rewound point. The three scenario-meanings user-observable signals: (1) session stays ready after the rewind (the fork is not a turn), asserted as a post_fork_ready dwell; (2) a linked NEW session_id appears (codex forks rather than rewinding in place like claudecode) — asserted as v2_session_birth new_session:true, the strongest observable that the conversation was rewound into a fresh thread; (3) the post-fork turn drives a clean ready→working→ready arc on UUID-2. agent_supports=partial: /fork reverts conversation state only (prior file edits stay in the working tree; file-revert checkpoint restore is open request #11626/#12558, unshipped). Provenance caveat (not asserted, not a bug): the parser skips session_meta (parser.go:179-180) and nothing sets ParentSessionID, so the daemon surfaces the fork as an independent new session_id rather than a parent-linked child — the new row + arc are still observed, only the forked_from_id label is lost. KNOWN CAPTURE GAP (driver, same as codex/session-reset): drive-codex-interactive.sh caches the first rollout and curate-lifecycle-fixture.sh may filter the single-session curated fixture to UUID-1 only, in which case the v2_* phases assert what the daemon observes LIVE but may be absent from the curated fixture — surfacing as validation drift until the codex driver tracks /fork-spawned sessions (a developer task, not an observability limit).

text: Pre-session row (proc-<PID>) appears in ready within 1s of codex launch
After the first prompt lands, the rollout-keyed session appears in ready — proc-<PID> hands off to a fresh UUID-1
First user prompt ('one') drives ready → working on the rollout session
First turn completes back to ready (same rollout UUID-1) — checkpoint 1
Second user prompt ('two') drives ready → working on the same rollout UUID-1
Second turn completes back to ready (same rollout UUID-1) — checkpoint 2, the point to fork back from

Spec: 'Session row reflects the rewound state with state ready' — after /fork the session stays in ready (the fork itself is not a turn) and the original rollout lingers frozen (no transcript_removed).

Spec: 'a linked new session_id if the adapter forks' — /fork materializes a fresh rollout UUID-2 never seen before, restored from the rewound point; this is the strongest observable that codex forked the conversation into a new thread.

Spec: 'Subsequent turns start cleanly from the rewound point: ready → working' — the post-fork prompt ('three') drives the NEW session ready → working (must NOT match a stale working transition on UUID-1).
Spec: post-fork turn completes back to ready on the new rollout UUID-2 — a clean ready → working → ready arc from the rewound point; final live state is ready.
